questionText: Find the set of values of $k$ for which the equation $7\sin\theta + 24\cos\theta = k$ has at least one solution.

steps.description: State the range of the expression
steps.explanation: The expression takes every value between $-25$ and $25$ as $\theta$ varies.
A solution exists precisely when $k$ is an achievable value of the expression.
Any $k$ in this closed interval is attained, since sine is continuous and reaches every value in $[-1,1]$.

steps.workingLatex: k = \pm 25 \text{ give exactly one solution each}
steps.explanation: At the extremes the sine touches $\pm 1$ once per revolution, so those values still count as having a solution.
